title: Migrate CI to GitHub Actions and delete obsolete CI/config files (.travis.yml, stale CircleCI + RobustPerception paths)
summary: The build CI is still CircleCI (.circleci/config.yml) and an ancient, unused .travis.yml is still present, while the maintainers want everything on GitHub Actions. The issue also asks to 'delete the travis config file and any other unused files lying around'. There is NO build/test GitHub Actions workflow yet -- .github/workflows only contains tiobe-scan.yaml and issues.yml (issue-sync/scan, not CI). A beginner-friendly slice is: remove the dead .travis.yml (pins Go 1.9.x), and either port .circleci/config.yml to a .github/workflows/ci.yaml or remove it, plus fix the stale RobustPerception references left over from the original fork.
why_good_first_issue: Mostly file deletion plus authoring a small YAML workflow that mirrors the existing CircleCI steps (build nrpe_exporter.go, run a Nagios NRPE server, smoke-test). No application code changes needed for the cleanup slice. The maintainer explicitly requested it. Clear 'done': travis/circleci leftovers gone and a working GH Actions build (or, for the smallest PR, just delete .travis.yml + correct the stale paths).
repro_steps: Go is NOT installed on VM (command -v go -> GO_ABSENT), so inspection tier. On VM cos: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/nrpe_exporter ~/work/nrpe_exporter; then `find .github -type f`, `cat .travis.yml`, `grep -n '' .circleci/config.yml`, `grep -n '' .promu.yml`, `grep -n module go.mod`.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: .github/workflows contains ONLY tiobe-scan.yaml and issues.yml (no build/test CI), so CircleCI is still the de-facto CI and the migration is NOT done. .travis.yml still present and pins `go: 1.9.x` + `master` (obsolete). .circleci/config.yml uses `image: circleci/golang:1.9` and `working_directory: /go/src/github.com/robustperception/nrpe_exporter` (old GOPATH path, wrong owner). .promu.yml:4 still has `path: github.com/RobustPerception/nrpe_exporter` whereas go.mod:1 is `module github.com/canonical/nrpe_exporter` -- so the promu {{repoPath}} used in version ldflags is stale. All confirm the issue is live and actionable.
fix_hint: Smallest viable PR: `git rm .travis.yml` and correct .promu.yml:4 to `github.com/canonical/nrpe_exporter`. Fuller PR: add .github/workflows/ci.yaml that checks out, sets up Go (actions/setup-go using go.mod's 1.17+), runs `go build nrpe_exporter.go` and `go vet ./...`, optionally replicating the CircleCI nagios-nrpe-server smoke test; then `git rm -r .circleci`. Update README if it references CircleCI badges (it does not currently).
notes: Self-identified extra cruft a contributor can fold in: the stale RobustPerception/robustperception paths in .promu.yml:4 and .circleci/config.yml:6 are exactly the 'unused/leftover files' the issue mentions. Go absent so CI cannot be exercised locally; evidence is file:line inspection. This repo's issues are unlabeled, but this is a textbook beginner cleanup; suggested label 'good first issue'.

title: Dead/unreachable log statement after http.ListenAndServe in main (nrpe_exporter.go:210)
summary: In main(), nrpe_exporter.go:206-211 wraps the server startup as `if err := http.ListenAndServe(*listenAddress, nil); err != nil { ...; os.Exit(1) }` followed by `level.Info(logger).Log("msg", "Listening on address", "address", *listenAddress)` on line 210. That final log line is unreachable: http.ListenAndServe blocks for the lifetime of the server when it succeeds, and the only way it returns is with a non-nil error, which is handled inside the if-block and ends in os.Exit(1). So execution never falls through to line 210. It is also redundant -- the same 'Listening on address' message is already logged at line 188 before the server starts. This is dead code that misleads readers into thinking a post-serve message is emitted.
why_good_first_issue: Tiny, self-contained, zero-risk fix in one file (delete one unreachable line). Requires only basic understanding that ListenAndServe blocks. Good for a first contribution to a Go repo: teaches the contributor about blocking servers and dead-code while making a real (if small) correctness/readability improvement. Clear 'done': the unreachable line is removed (and the real 'listening' log at line 188 retained).
repro_steps: Go is NOT installed on VM (GO_ABSENT), inspection tier. On VM cos: clone repo, then `grep -n '' nrpe_exporter.go` and read lines 186-211 (the main() server-start block). Compare the 'Listening on address' log at line 188 (before serving) with the one at line 210 (after the blocking if-block).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: nrpe_exporter.go:206 `if err := http.ListenAndServe(*listenAddress, nil); err != nil {`, line 207-208 logs error then `os.Exit(1)`, line 209 closes the if, line 210 `level.Info(logger).Log("msg", "Listening on address", "address", *listenAddress)`. Since ListenAndServe only returns on error and that branch calls os.Exit(1), line 210 can never execute. The identical message already exists at line 188 (`level.Info(logger).Log("msg", "Listening on address", "address", *listenAddress)`) right after the build-context logs, so line 210 is both unreachable and duplicated.
fix_hint: Delete nrpe_exporter.go:210 (the post-ListenAndServe `level.Info(...).Log("msg", "Listening on address", ...)`). The pre-serve announcement at line 188 already covers it. If the maintainers prefer, the error branch could instead `level.Error(...).Log("msg", "Error starting HTTP server", "err", err)` (currently line 207 logs the message without the err value) -- a nice optional adjacent cleanup.
notes: While reading this file two more low-risk beginner cleanups are visible if a contributor wants more scope: (a) line 207 logs 'Error starting HTTP server' but drops the actual err value (no 'err', err) so the cause is lost; (b) line 71 `ipaddr, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(...)` reassigns err but never checks it. These are optional; the unreachable line 210 is the cleanest single-line pick. Go absent -> verified by inspection, not `go vet` (go vet would also flag the unreachable code).
